evidence: The paper uses permutation variable importance (VIMP), not impurity-based importance, with 155 VIMP references and zero references to Gini/MDI. Authors report KDPI as having "second highest importance" (ranking) and conclude it "plays an important role in post-transplant survival, regardless of correlations with other predictors." They corroborate this ranking using conditional residual-based analysis. Since impurity importance bias does not apply to VIMP methods, p_affected is false.
